message:
fix(pool): replace expect() panics with proper poisoned mutex handling

Replace all `.expect("pool lock poisoned")` calls with graceful error handling:

- Add `lock_or_error()` helper method that returns Error::Pool(PoolError::poisoned(...))
  instead of panicking when the mutex is poisoned
- Update acquire() to propagate poisoning errors to callers via Outcome::Err
- Cleanup paths (acquire_cleanup, validate_cleanup) now use if-let to
  decrement counts only when lock succeeds - prevents cascading panics
- Wait paths (acquire_wait, acquire_wake, acquire_timeout) handle poisoning
  gracefully with tracing::error logging
- close() now recovers from poisoned mutex while still waking all waiters
  and marking pool as closed - ensures clean shutdown even after panics

This prevents the entire application from crashing when a single thread
panics while holding the pool lock. The pool remains usable for other
threads, and errors are properly reported to callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
